regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST, and it is UNCHANGED over the weekend: a healthy but EXTENDED bull near all-time highs — SPY bull-stacked (20>50>200), only −0.35% off its 52-week high, +9.24% over a rising 200-day, with 126/252-day momentum +10.06% / +22.31%. Today is Sat 7/11: the cash market is CLOSED and Tier-A re-ran on the SAME 7/10 close, so nothing moved run-over-run — the whole read is the WEEK AHEAD. Two features sit ON TOP of the intact bull. (1) A TOOL-ALIGNED leadership shift: the structural leaders TECH and SEMIS are COOLING on the 2-week RS accel — XLK Weakening ↓ / rotation:out (accel −0.4) and SOXX Weakening ↓ / rotation:out (accel −1.2) — even though both are bull-stacked with the board's strongest 3-/6-month RS (XLK 3m +19.6 / SOXX +51.5% over its 200-day); financials are the lone clean Leading sector (XLF rotation:into), the MAGS complex shows the strongest positive acceleration (Improving ↑, accel +2.51), and defensives/industrials are rolling over (XLV Peaking ⚠, XLI Weakening ↓ accel −3.48). (2) The one genuine STRUCTURAL crack: MSFT — bear-stacked, −28.51% off its high and −12.62% below its 200-day — still IDIOSYNCRATIC (QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacked near highs). PERSISTENCE-GATE caveat: the coarse Leading/Weakening labels have flip-flopped across the last three snapshots (the exact whipsaw the gate guards) — so do NOT trade the cooling as a rotation OUT of tech; anchor structure-first (bull-stacks + 3/6-month RS intact) and read rotation:out as 'HOLD, don't chase/add,' not 'sell.' All under a HAWKISH-HOLD Fed (7/29 ~79% hold / ~15-18% HIKE, no 2026 cuts). The week: CPI + heavy bank open 7/14 → PPI 7/15 → retail + claims 7/16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC + MSFT 7/29. WEEK-AHEAD posture: STAY LONG the structural bull; HOLD (don't chase) cooling tech/semis; treat XLF as an event-gated swing into bank earnings; TRIM the rolling-over defensives/industrials (XLV/XLI); WATCH — don't chase — the MAGS/XLC/XLY/XLE early-accumulation; carry a CHEAP defined-risk hedge into the CPI cluster (VIX 15.03, weekend gap unpriced); and do NOT call an MSFT bottom until it reclaims its 50-day (~403).

backdrop.pricedIn: The tape prices a BENIGN path: a cool/in-line June CPI (7/14) with the oil tail fading, a constructive big-bank kickoff, an orderly OPEX pin, and a HOLD at the 7/29 FOMC (~79% priced) — but the curve ALSO carries a ~15-18% HIKE tail and NO 2026 cuts, so 'benign' means 'no upside inflation surprise,' not 'easing coming.' Positioning is COMPLACENT — VIX 15.03 (its lowest read in a 7-print decline), SPY −0.35% off its all-time high — with NO weekend gap premium built ahead of Tuesday's CPI. The bar reality must clear: no hot inflation surprise that re-arms the hike tail.
backdrop.asymmetry: Near all-time highs, complacent (VIX 15.03, still falling), extended above trend, into the densest event window of the month (CPI + heavy bank open 7/14 → PPI 7/15 → retail + claims 7/16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC + MSFT 7/29) under a hawkish-hold Fed carrying a live hike tail — risk/reward skews to a DOWNSIDE catalyst against a market carrying almost no fear premium and no weekend gap hedge. Protection is cheap; the crowd is offside SHORT vol into KNOWN catalysts. The one genuinely cheap structural asset is the thing left for dead — MSFT (−28.51% off its high) — but only a 50-day reclaim (~403) confirms mean-reversion over a dead-cat bounce.

psychology.read: REGIME (named): a calm, positive-gamma, COMPLACENT tech-led bull near all-time highs with COOLING leadership breadth. SPY closed 7/10 bull-stacked (20>50>200), only −0.35% off its 52-week high, +9.24% over a rising 200-day, mom252 +22.31%; VIX 15.03 — the LOWEST read in a 7-print decline (…16.05→16.04→15.84→15.03). Today is Sat 7/11, the cash market is CLOSED, and Tier-A re-ran on the SAME 7/10 close, so NOTHING moved run-over-run — this is a WEEK-AHEAD read, not a reaction to a session. The composite score reads 0 only because Fear&Greed was unfetchable (VIX-only); the STRUCTURAL read is clearly bull. Two features sit ON TOP of the intact bull. (1) A TOOL-ALIGNED leadership rotation is in motion on the 2-week RS accel: the structural leaders TECH and SEMIS are COOLING (XLK Weakening ↓ / rotation:out accel −0.4; SOXX Weakening ↓ / rotation:out accel −1.2) even though both are bull-stacked with the board's strongest 3-/6-month RS; FINANCIALS are the lone clean Leading sector (XLF rotation:into, but negative 3m/6m RS = fresh, not entrenched); the MAGS complex shows the STRONGEST positive acceleration on the board (Improving ↑ / into, accel +2.51) though only 1/7 members are bull-stacked; and DEFENSIVES/INDUSTRIALS are rolling over (XLV Peaking ⚠, XLI Weakening ↓ accel −3.48). (2) One genuine structural CRACK — MSFT, bear-stacked, −28.51% off its high and −12.62% below its 200-day, the AI-capex 'show-me' (software) de-rate, still IDIOSYNCRATIC (QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacked near highs). CROWD POSTURE: complacent and short-vol into a KNOWN dense catalyst cluster that STARTS TUESDAY (June CPI + a heavy bank open 7/14 → PPI 7/15 → retail + claims 7/16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC + MSFT 7/29), under a hawkish-hold Fed (no 2026 cuts, a ~15-18% hike tail) that gives a hot inflation print more bite — with NO weekend gap premium built ahead of Tuesday. FALSIFIERS of this read: (1) SPY losing its 50-day (~740) = first structural crack; (2) MSFT's weakness SPREADING to QQQ/VGT/SPMO (idiosyncratic → systematic) = the real topping tell — OR MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~403) = the de-rate repairing; (3) the tech/semis cooling turning into a real 3-month-RS roll = upgrades 'HOLD, don't add' to an actual rotation-out; (4) VIX inverting to backwardation (IVTS >1.0); (5) a HOT June CPI (7/14) that re-arms the hike tail and breaks the OPEX level. NET week-ahead posture: STAY LONG the structural bull; HOLD (don't chase) the cooling tech/semis; treat XLF as an event-gated swing into the bank open; TRIM the rolling-over XLV/XLI; WATCH (don't chase) the MAGS/XLC/XLY/XLE early-accumulation; carry a CHEAP hedge into the CPI cluster; and do NOT treat MSFT's mild improvement as a bottom until it reclaims the 50-day.

The rotation story is TOOL-ALIGNED off the 2-week RS accel, NOT the raw 1-month RS level: leaders (XLK/SOXX) are rotation:OUT = HOLD/don't-add (they're bull-stacked with intact 3/6-month RS, and the coarse label whipsawed across the last 3 snapshots = do-nothing), XLF is the lone Leading/into event-gated swing, MAGS is the strongest positive accel but 1/7 bull-stacked = watch, and XLV/XLI are rotation:out = trim. experimental.title: Dead-cat vs repair: counter-trend bounce durability in a structurally-broken mega-cap (idea)
experimental.note: Method: when a bear-stacked name below both its 50- and 200-day prints a momentum improvement (this run: MSFT mom63 turned +3.45 while mom126/252 stay −19.99% / −22.91%, off-high −28.51%), classify it as a nascent bounce and base-rate the forward outcome — does it RECLAIM the 50-day (structural repair, tradeable long) or FAIL below it (dead-cat, the downtrend resumes)? Condition the base rate on (a) whether the name's own sector ETF is bull-stacked near highs (a supportive backdrop — this run VGT/semis are) and (b) distance to the 50-day (this run ~403, −4.52% overhead). Turns 'MSFT bounced' from a headline into a falsifiable, level-anchored call (reclaim vs reject). Inputs: brief-history.jsonl per-name mom5/mom21/mom63 runs + Tier-A maStack/ma50Dist/pctFrom52wHigh + the sector ETF's structure (all have). Directly frames this run's 'do not call the MSFT bottom until it reclaims the 50-day.' Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Mega-cap structural-breakdown vs index-at-highs divergence detector (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when a top-weight name is in a structural DOWNTREND (bear-stack, below its 200-day, deeply off its 52-week high) WHILE the index / its own sector ETF is bull-stacked near highs (this run: MSFT −12.62% below its 200-day, −28.51% off its high, bear-stack; QQQ/VGT bull-stacked near highs). Quantify the divergence (name 200-day distance − ETF 200-day distance, plus the 52-week-range gap) and base-rate the forward outcome: does the name mean-revert UP to the index (idiosyncratic, buy the eventual 50-day reclaim) or does the index roll over TO the name (systematic top, since the biggest weight leads the cap-weighted index)? Inputs: the Tier-A structural slice (maStack, ma200Dist, pctFrom52wHigh) — computed per name + the ETFs. This run it remains the single most important structural read. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Tool-aligned RRG rotation vs raw-RS-level divergence classifier (idea)
experimental.note: Method: for each sector/group, compare the RRG state/accel (2-week RS acceleration) against the raw 1-month RS LEVEL and flag DIVERGENCES where a naive level-reader would draw the opposite conclusion from the tool. Live proofs this run: XLK 1m RS turned POSITIVE (+0.2) yet RRG is Weakening ↓ / rotation:out (accel −0.4) — a level-reader would 'add,' the tool says 'HOLD/don't add'; XLE has the WORST 1m RS on the board (−6.03) yet RRG Improving ↑ / rotation:into (accel +0.68) — the tool flags an early turn a level-reader would avoid. Base-rate which signal (accel-direction vs level) better predicts forward RS. Inputs: the Tier-A rsMom1m/3m/6m + accel + rrgState + rotation per sector (all emitted). Directly enforces this run's 'author rotation from RRG accel, NOT the raw RS level' mandate. Promote if recurring — no owning tool contrasts the two today.

experimental.title: Fresh-vs-entrenched rotation classifier: 1-month RS leading while 3/6-month RS lag (idea)
experimental.note: Method: classify a sector's leadership as FRESH (1-month RS positive/Leading but 3- and 6-month RS negative — this run: XLF, 1m +3.87 but 3m −2.41 / 6m −9.01) vs ENTRENCHED (all three horizons positive — this run: XLK is the structural RS leader, 3m +19.6 / 6m +17.03, even while its 2-week accel cools). Base-rate the forward outcome: does a fresh rotation persist (3-month RS climbs >0 = durable handoff) or fade (1-month RS rolls back = head-fake)? Turns 'rotation' from a one-number snapshot into a horizon-aware, falsifiable call. Inputs: the Tier-A rsMom1m/3m/6m per sector (emitted). Directly frames this run's 'trade XLF as a swing, not a regime change.' Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Persistence-gated RS-change detector (noise vs signal) (idea)
experimental.note: Method: for each momentum/RS series, compute the tail consecutive-same-direction run across the last N brief-history snapshots (the rlbrief.js consecutiveRun/isPersistentSignal helpers) and only surface a 'change' when the run is >= minRun OR a structural level breaks. This run is a live proof: the coarse Leading/Weakening sector labels flip-flopped across the last 3 snapshots (pre-close/after-hours/morning) = a whipsaw with no clean tail run = NOISE, so 'tech rotated out' is not actionable on the coarse label, whereas VIX's 7-read monotonic decline = a genuine persistent signal and MSFT's multi-read bear-stack = a persistent structural state. Base-rate the hit-rate of persistence-gated vs raw single-slice signals to quantify how much whipsaw the gate removes. Inputs: brief-history.jsonl (have) + the pure helpers (have, selftest-covered). Promote to a visible 'signal quality' badge if recurring.

experimental.title: Complacency-into-catalyst gauge: persistent VIX decline + event density (idea)
experimental.note: Method: combine a PERSISTENT VIX decline (this run: 16.9→16.74→16.57→16.05→16.04→15.84→15.03, a 7-read fall to the lowest print) with a forward event-density count over the next ~10 trading days (this run: CPI + heavy bank open 7/14 + PPI + retail + OPEX + FOMC/MSFT = very dense) and the index's distance from its ATH (−0.35%) to score a 'priced-for-perfection' complacency reading — the setup where cheap protection has the best risk/reward, sharpened here by an unhedged WEEKEND gap into Tuesday's CPI. Base-rate forward drawdown conditioned on high-complacency-into-high-event-density. Inputs: ^VIX series + macroEvents[] + Tier-A pctFrom52wHigh (all have). Frames this run's 'carry a cheap hedge into the catalyst cluster' call. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: VIX term-structure regime flag: contango->backwardation cross (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track IVTS = VIX/VIX3M (and VIX9D/VIX) daily; flag the cross of IVTS through 1.0 (contango->backwardation) as a regime-change trigger, with day-count of the current regime and a base-rate study of forward SPX returns / realized-vol after each cross. This run's whole calm read hinges on the curve STAYING in contango (IVTS ~0.85 carried); backwardation is the named falsifier. Inputs: ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M (add VIX9D/3M to the Tier-A fetch). Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Hawkish-hold hike-tail asymmetry gauge: how a macro print transmits when cut-odds are ~zero (idea)
experimental.note: Method: condition the market's reaction function on the FOMC-priced path, not just the print's surprise. This run the 7/28-29 FOMC (non-SEP) carries ~79% HOLD, only ~2% cut, and a LIVE ~15-18% HIKE tail (CME FedWatch / Kalshi, web-corroborated 7/11 — re-verify live), with no 2026 cuts and a hawkish Chair. In that regime a hot CPI does NOT merely delay cuts (there are none to delay) — it RE-ARMS a hike tail, so the downside convexity of an inflation upside-surprise is larger than in a cut-path regime, even at the same surprise magnitude. Build a 'hike-tail bite' score = f(priced hike prob, priced cut prob, distance-to-ATH, VIX complacency) that scales the recommended hedge size ahead of a CPI/PPI. Base-rate forward SPX drawdown on hot-CPI prints split by whether the curve carried a live hike tail vs priced cuts. Inputs: CME FedWatch priced path (web, verify each run) + macroEvents[] + ^VIX + Tier-A pctFrom52wHigh. Directly frames this run's sharper hot-CPI asymmetry. Promote if recurring — no owning tool prices the Fed-path-conditioned reaction function today.
